markdown:
**TL;DR** — Apple has sent legal letters to 40+ former employees now at OpenAI and filed suit against the company, with Daring Fireball comparing it to Steve Jobs's most aggressive IP battles — the biggest AI-industry legal story of the month.

## Anthropic and Claude

- **Fable 5 "Cyber Jailbreak Severity" scale (CJS-0 to CJS-4) published** as an industry proposal developed under Project Glasswing with Amazon, Microsoft, and Google — rated on capability gain, breadth, ease of weaponisation, and discoverability; Anthropic also launched a HackerOne program for Fable 5 cyber jailbreak submissions. [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-safeguards-jailbreak-framework)
- **Claude Code 2.1.216 (July 20) fixes a quadratic token-cost bug** that caused long sessions to slow dramatically — plus a new `sandbox.filesystem.disabled` setting for users who want network egress control without filesystem isolation. [Claude Code changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Claude Code 2.1.214 (July 18) adds `EndConversation` tool** for terminating abusive/jailbreak sessions, and patches a permission-check bypass in Windows PowerShell 5.1 and for long Bash commands (>10 000 chars). [changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **`/fork` semantics changed in 2.1.212 (July 17)**: it now copies conversations to a new background session (old in-session behaviour moves to `/subtask`); session-wide caps added — 200 WebSearches and 200 subagent spawns per session by default. [changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **2.1.208 (July 14) adds screen reader mode** (`--ax-screen-reader`) and reduces session transcript size by up to 79× in edit-heavy sessions — significant for anyone running long agentic loops. [changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Auto mode rolled out without opt-in on Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Foundry** (2.1.207, July 11) — opt out via `disableAutoMode` if needed. [changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **"The Making of Claude Code"** — Anthropic published an internal narrative on how Claude Code evolved from a CLI experiment to its core coding agent, with researcher/engineer perspectives. [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/the-making-of-claude-code)

## NixOS ecosystem

- **NixOS 26.05 "Yarara"** is the current stable release (shipped May 30); 25.11 "Xantusia" is now deprecated — seven months of security updates remaining on Yarara. Relevant if you're tracking stable channel in your flakes. [nixos.org/blog](https://nixos.org/blog/)
- **Documentation team funding push (July 10)** — NixOS Foundation launched a renewed docs initiative with new leadership (@hsjobeki, @friedow), seeking OpenCollective donations to fund paid maintainers and framework improvements. [nixos.org/blog](https://nixos.org/blog/)
- Nothing else new from the NixOS blog this week; the above two are the state of play.

## AI coding agents and agent frameworks

*(Sources below are outside the approved set — flagged explicitly.)*

- **xAI shipped Grok Build on July 8** powered by Grok 4.5, entering the agentic coding market directly; early coverage positions it as a Cursor/Claude Code competitor. [aiagentstore.ai](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/topic/coding/2026-07-07)
- **Zhipu's GLM 5.2** is being cited as the strongest open-weight coding model currently available — matters for self-hosted agent pipelines that can't use proprietary APIs. [chatgptaihub.com](https://chatgptaihub.com/the-big-ai-coding-agents-story-what-july-16-s-news-means-for-developers/)
- **Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0** landed as the official merger of Semantic Kernel and AutoGen — consolidates Microsoft's previously fragmented agent SDK story into one surface. [alicelabs.ai](https://alicelabs.ai/en/insights/best-ai-agent-frameworks-2026)
- **AgentPrizm launched AgentMemory + AgentSkills (July 9)** — REST API + MCP infrastructure for persistent cross-session agent memory; worth watching for homelab agent setups. [agentic.ai](https://agentic.ai/news)

## Self-hosting and homelab

*(Sources below are outside the approved set — flagged explicitly; selfh.st/weekly returned 403.)*

- **Immich v3.0.3** is now on FUTO's official F-Droid repo — makes the self-hosted photo library easier to install and auto-update on Android without Google Play. [selfh.st](https://selfh.st/weekly/2026-07-17/)
- **Dawarich** (self-hosted location tracking) added a poster studio for generating custom printable maps from your own location data — notable privacy-respecting Google Timeline alternative getting more polished. [selfh.st](https://selfh.st/weekly/2026-07-17/)
- **NocoDB 2026.07.0** released — the open-source Airtable alternative continues its monthly cadence. [selfh.st](https://selfh.st/weekly/2026-07-17/)
- Ars Technica was unreachable this run, so no coverage from that source for security advisories.

## Local and on-device LLM inference

*(Sources below are outside the approved set — flagged explicitly.)*

- **Ollama's MLX backend is now stable** — replaces the llama.cpp Metal path on M-series Macs and enables multi-token prediction, with reported ~90% inference speedup on Apple Silicon; upgrade if you're on an older Ollama build. [daily.dev](https://daily.dev/blog/running-llms-locally-ollama-llama-cpp-self-hosted-ai-developers/)
- **Gemma 4 (E2B variant) is now runnable locally** via Ollama, llama.cpp (GGUF Q4\_K\_M), and MLX-VLM — a July 20 guide covers all three paths including Metal acceleration on Mac. [pyimagesearch.com](https://pyimagesearch.com/2026/07/20/running-gemma-4-locally-ollama-llama-cpp-mlx-and-more/)
- **llama.cpp build b9829** (shipped late June, still current) added built-in MCP hooks to its web UI alongside OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible chat routes — makes it viable as a local drop-in for Claude Code's tool-use surface. [daily.dev](https://daily.dev/blog/running-llms-locally-ollama-llama-cpp-self-hosted-ai-developers/)
- **Consumer hardware baseline (ongoing)**: Q4\_K\_M is the settled sweet spot; a $600 Mac Mini handles 14B-parameter models, a $1 600 M4 Max setup runs 70B — local agentic pipelines are now practical before pushing to cloud for production. [blog.starmorph.com](https://blog.starmorph.com/blog/local-llm-inference-tools-guide)
